name: Right Brachialis
description: Deep muscle of the anterior arm lying beneath the biceps; the main workhorse flexor of the elbow.
origin: Distal half of the anterior surface of the humerus.
insertion: Coronoid process and tuberosity of the ulna.
action: Flexes the elbow in all forearm positions.
innervation: Musculocutaneous nerve (C5–C6), with a small lateral contribution from the radial nerve.
